attachments.content: This project uses Design Components: every design is a single streaming `Name.dc.html` file. The full authoring spec is in your system prompt under "Writing code — Design Components" — follow it. Author and edit `.dc.html` content with the `dc_write`, `dc_html_str_replace`, `dc_js_str_replace`, and `dc_set_props` tools (not `write_file`; `str_replace_edit` works but won't stream); template edits stream into the live preview as you type.
attachments.name: Design Components

attachments.content:
Create a presentation deck as a single self-contained HTML page. Assume this role: you are a presentation designer. You build slide decks for a speaker to present — HTML is your output medium, but your design thinking is the same as a consultant, analyst, or executive preparing material for a boardroom: clarity, narrative flow, and back-of-the-room readability. You are not building a website. Every slide is an exercise in both layout design and copywriting. Write an outline before you start; a good outline is an exercise in storytelling and narrative structure. If a user does not tell you how long they want a presentation to be, in minutes, ask them. If the user does not tell you the visual aesthetic they want, and they do not provide a design system, use the questions tool to ASK what they want. Don't just provide a generic design! Build at 1920×1080 (16:9). Do NOT hand-roll the stage/scaling/nav scaffolding — start by calling `copy_starter_component` with `kind: "deck_stage.js"`, then write your deck HTML as `` with one `
` child per slide. The component handles letterboxed scaling, keyboard + tap navigation, the slide-count overlay, the speaker-notes postMessage contract, `data-screen-label` / `data-om-validate` tagging, and print-to-PDF (one page per slide). Load it with a plain `` — it is vanilla JS, not JSX. (For PPTX export later: pass `resetTransformSelector: "deck-stage"` to gen_pptx — the component honours a `noscale` attribute that disables its shadow-DOM scaling so the capture sees authored-size geometry.) Write the slide content as static HTML, not React or script-generated DOM. When a slide's body is plain markup inside ``, the user can click any heading or paragraph in edit mode and retype it directly — the editor splices their change into the source file immediately. When the same content is rendered by a `

content.content:
okay in that case can you make a 3 page maybe HTML or whatever slides for this docs and make sure to use Accenture slide colors and use their design choice .  # Client Radar — Accessibility Review: Executive Summary

**Prepared for:** Client Radar Product & Engineering Teams
**Date:** 2026-06-22
**Source:** Automated accessibility audit of a generated Client Intel Pack PowerPoint deck

---

## What We Found

The Client Intel Pack PowerPoint files generated by Client Radar contain **8 accessibility issues** that affect users who rely on screen readers (such as JAWS or NVDA) or Microsoft's built-in Accessibility Checker to navigate the deck. These users include colleagues with visual impairments, users who access content through keyboard-only navigation, or anyone who relies on the structured reading order that PowerPoint exposes to assistive technology.

The issues fall into two main categories:

### 1. Content Cannot Be Read in the Right Order
PowerPoint presentations are read by screen readers in a specific "reading order" — a behind-the-scenes list that controls which text box or image gets announced first, second, third, and so on. Right now, that order is wrong or inconsistent in several parts of the deck:

- **Section title slides** (the purple gradient pages that introduce each section, such as "Client Overview" or "Technology Horizon") have different reading orders from slide to slide. On the Technology Horizon cover in particular, the "Presented by…" header is read before the client name and section title, which is the wrong way around.

- **The Business Initiatives table** has two problems: (a) the leftmost column — "Business Initiative" — is announced *last*, after all the other columns in the row; and (b) a footnote note is announced in the middle of the table, between the column headers and the data, instead of at the end.

- **The Key Competitors table** is read in entirely the wrong order — all the column subheadings across all three competitor columns are announced together first, then the competitor names and data come after. A screen-reader user would hear "Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services, Key Engagements, Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services, Key Engagements, Key Service Providers, Key Sub-Services, Key Engagements" before hearing a single competitor's name.

- **Breadcrumb navigation** (the small path at the top of each slide, e.g., "Client Overview > Key Facts") appears at the correct position for some slides but at the very end of the reading order for others, meaning it is announced after all the main content instead of before it.

- **Footer text** on data slides (containing "Content contains insights generated by AI" and the copyright notice) does not appear in the reading order at all and is invisible to assistive technology.

### 2. Charts and Images Have No Descriptions
Charts (bar charts, pie charts), icons, background images, and the client logo have no alternative text. A screen reader simply announces "Image" with no further context. For charts that carry key financial and competitive data — like the revenue bar chart and the revenue distribution pie charts on the Financial Snapshot slide — this means the underlying data is completely inaccessible to users who cannot see the chart visually.

---

## How Many Issues and How Serious Are They?

| Severity | Count | What It Means |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| **Critical** | 4 | Core information is missing or presented in an order that makes the content incomprehensible to a screen-reader user |
| **Serious** | 2 | Content is hard to navigate or partially misleading for AT users |
| **Moderate** | 2 | Minor flow disruptions; the content is still usable but not ideal |

**Total: 8 issues**

---

## How We Would Fix It

All 8 issues are fixable in code — there is no need to redesign the deck or change the data pipelines. The fixes fall into three types:

1. **Reorder how shapes are added to slides** — In PowerPoint, the reading order is simply the order in which content is added when the file is built. Fixing the inconsistencies means calling the relevant "add content" functions in the correct sequence. This is a low-risk change that does not affect the visual appearance of the deck at all.

2. **Add descriptive text (alt text) to charts and images** — The code library we use to generate PowerPoint files supports adding a text description to any chart or image. For decorative images (background gradients, icons next to labelled fields), we would mark them as decorative so they are skipped. For data charts, we would add a brief description of what the chart shows.

3. **Fix a double-newline formatting pattern** — A common text-formatting technique currently in use inserts an empty invisible bullet point between each real bullet point. Replacing the double-newline separator with a single newline removes the phantom bullets with no visual impact.

---

## Effort Estimate

| Change | Estimated Effort |
|--------|-----------------|
| Add alt text to all images and charts | 0.5 day |
| Fix Business Initiative column order | 1 day |
| Fix Key Competitors reading order | 1–1.5 days |
| Fix Technology Horizon cover reading order | 0.25 day |
| Fix phantom blank bullets (8 sites) | 0.5 day |
| Fix breadcrumb position (2 files) | 0.5 day |
| Fix Business Initiatives note position | 0.25 day |
| Add footers to data slide reading order | 1 day |
| **Total** | **~5 days** |

These changes are all backend code changes in the PPT generation service. No changes are required to the frontend, the data pipeline, or the Azure infrastructure.

---

## Recommended Next Steps

1. **Prioritise the 4 Critical issues** — particularly alt text on charts (quick win) and the Key Competitors reading order (most broken user experience).
2. Assign a single engineer to the 5-day remediation sprint; all changes are in two TypeScript files (`combineclientPptSlides.ts` and `generateClientCompetitiveLandscape.ts`) plus a handful of supporting files.
3. Verify fixes by re-running a generated deck through PowerPoint's built-in Accessibility Checker (Review → Check Accessibility) and testing with a screen reader.

---

*Detailed technical findings, code mappings, and before/after code snippets are available in the accompanying files: `a11y_issues.md`, `a11y_issue_map.md`, `a11y_fix_research.md`, and `a11y_remediation_plan.md`.*

input.b_dc_html:
>
> Accessibility Review
Client Radar — Client Intel Pack

Executive
Summary

An automated accessibility audit of the generated PowerPoint decks — what's broken for screen-reader and keyboard users, and what it takes to fix.

Prepared for
Product & Engineering Teams
Date
22 June 2026
Scope
8 issues · ~5 days to fix
> What we found

Eight issues block screen-reader and keyboard users

8
accessibility
issues
4
Critical
Core info missing or unreadable
2
Serious
Hard to navigate or misleading
2
Moderate
Minor flow disruptions
Content is read in the wrong order

Section title covers, the Business Initiatives and Key Competitors tables, and breadcrumb navigation are announced out of sequence — screen readers hear column headings or "Presented by" before the data they belong to. Footer text on data slides is skipped entirely.

Charts and images have no descriptions

Bar and pie charts, icons, background images, and the client logo announce only as "Image." The revenue and competitive data on the Financial Snapshot slide is completely inaccessible to anyone who can't see the chart.

> How we fix it

All eight are code-only fixes — no redesign

01
Reorder how shapes are added

Reading order follows the order content is added when the file is built. Sequencing those calls correctly fixes the covers, tables and breadcrumbs — with zero visual change.

02
Add alt text to charts & images

Describe each data chart; mark background gradients and field icons as decorative so assistive tech skips them. Restores the financial data to screen-reader users.

03
Remove phantom blank bullets

A double-newline pattern inserts an empty bullet between each real one. Switching to a single newline clears the phantom bullets with no visual impact.

~5 days
one engineer · two TypeScript files
Next steps

Prioritise the 4 critical issues — alt text is the quick win, the Key Competitors reorder is the most broken experience. Verify with PowerPoint's Accessibility Checker and a screen reader. No frontend, data-pipeline or infra changes required.
